gloss.en: having the skill or chance to do something

words.example: You must be able to speak French for this job.

gloss.en: different from what is normal or usual

words.example: The doctor found an abnormal heartbeat.

gloss.en: on or onto a ship, plane, or train

words.example: Welcome aboard the train to London.

gloss.en: an original native inhabitant of a country

words.example: The museum shows the art of the aborigine people.

gloss.en: at or to a higher place

words.example: Put it on the shelf above.

gloss.en: completely; used to emphasize something

words.example: You're absolutely right.

gloss.en: existing in large amounts; plentiful

words.example: The forest has abundant water and food.

gloss.en: the right or chance to use something

words.example: High-speed internet access has become a necessity.

gloss.en: an extra item added to something else

words.example: A scarf is a nice accessory for winter.

gloss.en: happening by chance, not on purpose

words.example: The fire was accidental, not planned.

gloss.en: to make yourself used to something

words.example: It took time to accustom myself to the cold.

gloss.en: a continuous dull pain

words.example: I have an ache in my back.

gloss.en: something done successfully through effort

words.example: Winning the prize was a great achievement.

gloss.en: the act of accepting that something is true

words.example: She gave a quick acknowledgement of his help.

gloss.en: the act of accepting that something is true

words.example: He nodded in acknowledgment of the news.

gloss.en: a person you know but not well

words.example: He is just an acquaintance, not a friend.

gloss.en: a particular thing that somebody does

words.example: You have committed a serious criminal act.

gloss.en: always busy doing things, especially physical

words.example: Staying physically active keeps you young.

gloss.en: a woman who performs in films or plays

words.example: The award-winning actress took a break from acting.

gloss.en: a notice telling people about a product

words.example: The TV ads were first run last year.

gloss.en: a thing that is added to something else

words.example: The new room is a nice addition to the house.

gloss.en: the person a letter is sent to

words.example: The addressee was not at home.

gloss.en: a feeling of respect and approval

words.example: I have great admiration for her work.

gloss.en: to respect somebody for what they do

words.example: I really admire your enthusiasm.

gloss.en: to agree that something is true

words.example: It was a stupid thing to do, I admit.

gloss.en: very attractive and easy to love

words.example: The baby is absolutely adorable.

gloss.en: having the most modern ideas or methods

words.example: Scientists work on highly advanced technology.

gloss.en: a word that describes a verb or adjective

words.example: "Quickly" is an adverb.

gloss.en: acting like or relating to an adverb

words.example: "At home" is an adverbial phrase.

gloss.en: a notice telling people about a product

words.example: I saw a funny advert on television.

gloss.en: to tell the public about a product

words.example: If you want customers you need to advertise.

gloss.en: to tell somebody what they should do

words.example: I would strongly advise against going alone.

gloss.en: a person who gives advice

words.example: She is a financial adviser at the bank.

gloss.en: a person who gives advice

words.example: My advisor helped me choose courses.

gloss.en: energetic exercises done to music

words.example: She does aerobics every morning.

gloss.en: to produce a change in something

words.example: How will these changes affect us?

gloss.en: to have enough money to buy something

words.example: Can we afford a new car?

gloss.en: at a later time; after an event

words.example: We had dinner and talked afterward.

gloss.en: the number of years a person has lived

words.example: You're the same age as my brother.

gloss.en: of a particular age

words.example: They have two children aged six and nine.

gloss.en: a person who acts for others in business

words.example: Our agent in New York deals with US sales.

gloss.en: an arrangement or promise made with somebody

words.example: They signed an international peace agreement.

gloss.en: further forward in space or time; in front

words.example: I'll run ahead and warn them.

gloss.en: the purpose of doing something; a goal

words.example: The main aim of the study was clear.

gloss.en: a company that provides regular flights

words.example: He works as an airline pilot.

gloss.en: a book for photos, or a music record

words.example: She showed me her photo album.

gloss.en: drinks such as beer and wine

words.example: He never drinks alcohol.

gloss.en: containing or connected with alcohol

words.example: They serve alcoholic drinks at the party.

gloss.en: having a bad reaction to something

words.example: I am allergic to cats.

gloss.en: in a voice that can be heard

words.example: Please read the sentence aloud.

gloss.en: the set of letters used in a language

words.example: The English alphabet has 26 letters.

gloss.en: that can be used instead of something else

gloss.en: very surprised

words.example: I was amazed by the news.

gloss.en: very surprising, in a way you admire

words.example: She told us an amazing story.

gloss.en: something you very much want to achieve

words.example: She had fulfilled her lifelong ambition.

gloss.en: strongly wanting success or power

words.example: He is a fiercely ambitious young manager.

gloss.en: to examine something closely to understand it

words.example: The job involves collecting and analysing data.

gloss.en: a detailed study of something to understand it

words.example: They carried out a careful data analysis.

gloss.en: to examine something closely to understand it

words.example: We need to analyze the test results.

gloss.en: full of life; made using animation

words.example: The children watched an animated film.

gloss.en: to tell people something officially

words.example: He officially announced his plan to resign.

gloss.en: a statement that informs people about something

words.example: The manager will make an announcement soon.

gloss.en: to make somebody slightly angry

words.example: His constant joking began to annoy her.

gloss.en: a feeling of slight anger

words.example: She frowned in annoyance at the noise.

gloss.en: slightly angry

words.example: He was annoyed with me about my carelessness.

gloss.en: making somebody feel slightly angry

words.example: This interruption is very annoying.

gloss.en: a small insect that lives in groups

words.example: An ant carried the crumb away.

gloss.en: opposed to something or someone

words.example: He is strongly anti war.

gloss.en: a word with the opposite meaning

words.example: "Hot" is an antonym of "cold".

gloss.en: in a worried or nervous way

words.example: She waited anxiously for the results.

gloss.en: in any case; anyway

words.example: It's late, but I'll finish it anyhow.

gloss.en: separated by distance in space or time

words.example: The two houses stood 500 metres apart.

gloss.en: a large animal like a monkey without a tail

gloss.en: to say you are sorry for something

words.example: Why should I apologize?

gloss.en: the sound of people clapping their hands

words.example: The singer received loud applause.

gloss.en: a formal request for a job or place

words.example: She sent in her visa application.

gloss.en: a formal arrangement to meet somebody

words.example: I've got a dental appointment at three.

gloss.en: to recognize the good qualities of something

words.example: He came to appreciate the beauty of the river.

gloss.en: almost but not completely accurate

gloss.en: a person whose job is designing buildings

gloss.en: the basic math of numbers and counting

words.example: She is good at arithmetic.

gloss.en: a large group of soldiers trained to fight

words.example: The two armies faced each other.

gloss.en: to plan or organize something in advance

words.example: The party was arranged quickly.

gloss.en: a plan or preparation you make in advance

gloss.en: the act of taking somebody into custody

gloss.en: the act of coming to a place

words.example: We apologize for the late arrival of the train.

gloss.en: a person who creates art, such as paintings

gloss.en: a common drug that reduces pain

words.example: I took an aspirin for my headache.

gloss.en: a task given to somebody as part of work

gloss.en: to help somebody to do something

words.example: Anyone willing to assist can call this number.

gloss.en: a scientist who studies the stars and planets

gloss.en: physically strong, fit, and active

gloss.en: relating to the Atlantic Ocean

gloss.en: the mixture of gases around the earth

gloss.en: relating to atoms or atomic energy

gloss.en: to fasten or join one thing to another

gloss.en: to be present at an event

words.example: We'd like as many people as possible to attend.

gloss.en: the way you think and feel about something

gloss.en: to make somebody interested or wanting something

gloss.en: an interesting place to go or thing to do

gloss.en: the power to give orders to people

gloss.en: giving extra help or support

gloss.en: a wide street in a town or city
